about.title: Understand business through data. Drive decisions with insight.
about.roleTags: Business Analytics · Data Science · Product Insight
about.para1: BBA from Jiangxi University of Finance and Economics, admitted to Nanjing University of Science and Technology for an MSc in Management Science and Engineering. Passionate about business data analysis and data-driven decision making.
about.para2: Skilled in SQL, Python, and Tableau for processing and analyzing complex business data, extracting actionable insights from large-scale information, and driving decisions through clear visualization.
about.para3: Aspiring to deepen expertise in business analytics and data science, combining management science with data analysis capabilities to create quantifiable business value.

project1.name: Beauty E-commerce: User Behavior & Conversion Analysis
project1.brief: Built recommendation-scenario conversion analysis based on beauty e-commerce user behavior logs
highlights: Designed PV, UV, CTR, CVR, GMV, AOV, and retention rate metrics for recommendation scenario conversion funnels
Used SQL for data cleaning, joins, and aggregation across user, product, behavior, and time dimensions
Conducted A/B experiment analysis with Python, comparing CTR, CVR, and GMV differences between groups
Built Tableau dashboards for recommendation effectiveness and user conversion, identified key drop-off points
project1.background: This project analyzes real user behavior logs from a beauty e-commerce platform, systematically examining user conversion paths within recommendation scenarios. Recommendation systems are the core traffic distribution mechanism for e-commerce platforms. Understanding user behavior patterns and conversion bottlenecks in recommendation scenarios is critical for optimizing recommendation strategies and increasing platform GMV.
project1.methodology: Following the CRISP-DM data analysis methodology, the project progresses from business understanding through data preparation, modeling, and evaluation. Stratified drill-down analysis decomposes conversion differences by user activity level, product category, and region. Two-sample Z-tests evaluate the statistical significance of conversion rate differences between experiment and control groups.
project1.callout: In A/B experiment analysis, Bonferroni correction is applied to address the multiple comparisons problem across multiple metrics, preventing false positive inflation from repeated testing and ensuring statistical reliability of experimental conclusions.
project1.edaText: Extracted five-level behavioral data (impression, click, favorite, add-to-cart, purchase) from raw logs using SQL. Completed missing value handling, outlier detection, and data consistency validation. Descriptive analysis revealed that recommendation scenarios contribute approximately 52% of platform traffic, yet overall conversion rate is only 68% of non-recommendation scenarios, indicating significant optimization potential.
vizInsights1: Five-level conversion funnel shows typical declining pattern, impression→click CTR approximately 4.2%
Click→add-to-cart is the largest drop-off point with 73% loss rate
Add-to-cart→purchase conversion is relatively stable, suggesting good price-product fit
vizInsights2: High-activity users contribute 58% of GMV but have lower recommendation conversion rates than medium-activity users
Tier-1 city users show significantly higher CTR than lower-tier cities, but purchase conversion differences are not significant
Beauty tool category shows highest recommendation efficiency; skincare has strongest cross-sell conversion
project1.statsText: Hypothesis testing via Python comparing experiment group (optimized algorithm) vs control group (original algorithm). Experiment group significantly outperformed on both CTR and CVR (p < 0.01), with GMV increase of approximately 12.3%. Effect size analysis (Cohen's d) indicates optimization benefits are most pronounced for medium-activity user segments.
project1.conclusion: Recommendation scenarios represent a core growth engine for e-commerce platforms with significant conversion optimization potential. Systematic improvements through refined user segmentation, optimized ranking strategies, and differentiated operations can enhance traffic monetization efficiency.
recommendations: Enhance recommendation diversity for high-activity users and strengthen cross-category recommendation capabilities
Establish lifecycle-stage-based tiered recommendation strategies with differentiated reach frequency and content
Improve product information richness in recommendation scenarios to reduce decision friction at click→add-to-cart stage

project2.name: Global Game Sales Analysis & Platform Strategy Research
project2.brief: Analyzed global game market trends, platform competition, and regional preferences using Kaggle sales data
highlights: Cleaned raw sales data using MySQL: handled outliers, missing values, year validation; unified 1980-2016 sample scope
Built KPIs: annual sales trends, platform lifecycle, platform×genre matrix, regional preference index, publisher market share
Created 3-page Tableau dashboard covering market overview, platform & genre analysis, regional & publisher analysis
Found global market peak in 2008-2009; PS2, X360, PS3 dominated; Japan showed strong Role-Playing preference
project2.background: Based on the Kaggle global game sales dataset covering over 16,000 game titles from 1980-2016 with information on sales, platforms, publishers, genres, and regional distribution. This analysis aims to provide data-driven insights for game product planning, platform selection, and market entry strategy.
project2.methodology: Employing descriptive statistics and exploratory data analysis (EDA) methods, the analysis framework covers four dimensions: temporal trends, platform lifecycles, genre matrices, and regional preferences. MySQL for data cleaning and KPI calculation, Tableau for interactive dashboards, and SPSS for significance testing of regional preference indices.
project2.callout: In platform lifecycle analysis, moving average smoothing is applied to reduce annual sales volatility, enabling clearer identification of complete lifecycle curves from introduction through growth to decline phases, providing reference for platform investment timing decisions.
project2.edaText: Data cleaning performed in MySQL: removed incomplete years post-2017, unified regional sales definitions (NA, EU, JP, Other), handled missing values and outliers. Final dataset contains 16,598 valid game records across 31 platforms, 12 genres, and 579 publishers.
vizInsights1: Global game market peaked in 2008-2009, followed by overall declining trend
PS2 leads with 1,231 titles, followed by X360 and PS3
Average platform lifecycle spans 8-10 years, with the first 3-4 years being the golden window for game releases
vizInsights2: Japan shows Role-Playing genre preference index of 2.8, significantly above global average
North American market favors Action and Sports genres with highest sales volume
Publisher market concentration follows long-tail distribution; Top 10 publishers hold approximately 45% market share
project2.statsText: Constructed Regional Genre Preference Index using standardized processing to eliminate overall sales volume differences. Japan's Role-Playing preference index reaches 2.8 (p < 0.001), Europe's Racing preference index at 1.6. Preference index significance validated via chi-square testing, providing statistical support for regional product strategies.
project2.conclusion: The global game market exhibits clear platform lifecycle patterns and regional genre preferences. Game publishing decisions should comprehensively consider target platform lifecycle stages, target market genre preference characteristics, and publisher competitive dynamics to formulate differentiated market entry and investment strategies.
recommendations: Prioritize platforms in early-to-mid growth phase as launch targets to maximize market window opportunities
Increase Role-Playing genre investment for Japanese market; prioritize Action and Sports genres for Western markets
Explore differentiation opportunities among long-tail publishers to establish first-mover advantages in niche genres
